topic: In China employee termination: myth-vs-fact guide with payroll cost-risk matrix for foreign employers

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Framework for lawful termination, severance obligations, and compensation exposure.
Supports requirement for defensible process and documentation consistency.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Confirms social insurance obligations relevant to termination-month payroll and closure steps.
Supports need for post-termination processing controls.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Provides practical tax context for final-pay component treatment and annual reconciliation environment.
Supports documenting assumptions for component-level payroll treatment.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Highlights process discipline and documentation as key multinational employer risk controls.
Reinforces execution-quality lens for China employment operations.

consensus: Termination risk is mostly execution risk: basis, payroll treatment, and communication alignment.
Documented controls and exception governance reduce dispute and rework exposure.
Foreign employers benefit from explicit risk-tier decisions before payroll release.
uncertainties: City-level interpretation and evidence thresholds can vary by fact pattern.
Some component-level treatment details may require local confirmation before payment release.
English-language source summaries may not capture all implementation details from Chinese-language materials.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: Use a risk-tier matrix (low/medium/high) to decide in-cycle payment scope, off-cycle handling, and escalation path for each termination case.
